action.reasoning: Observation: Training failed for 20+ iters (since ~iter 12) with persistent ModuleNotFoundError for xgboost (packages_to_install was never set in any prior JSON despite code in model.py/train.py/predict.py being fully ready: XGBRegressor with objective='reg:absoluteerror', tree_method='hist', n_estimators=1000, max_depth=9, lr=0.05, EarlyStopping(rounds=50) callback on val Morgan FP r=3/2048bit, metrics.jsonl logging per RAB_ITERATION, best.pkl save/load). Best MAE remains 0.2188 from RF (iter6); MLP was 0.231. Iter7 curve shows smooth convergence but FP+tree ensemble is capacity-limited. Analysis: xgboost is explicitly allowed, persists after one install via packages_to_install; with MAE objective + early_stop on val it should beat RF baseline (~0.15-0.18 MAE expected) on this high-dim sparse tabular QM9 task. FP calc (~4-6min) fits in remaining ~1.77h/2 iters. No code bugs (callback fixed, fast ConvertToNumpyArray used). Decision: Install xgboost, run current train.py on full CSV data (computes FPs, trains with callback+eval_set, logs val_mae, saves best.pkl), submit_eval on best.pkl for test MAE improvement + timing before exploring Mordred/3D GNNs if time allows.
